generated_assessment.reasoning: High conscientiousness and dutifulness (85th pct) would typically support defense spending as a matter of order, but this is strongly counterbalanced by very high openness (81st pct) and openness to values (77th pct) which often correlate with skepticism of military expansion, and exceptionally low anger/hostility (2nd pct) and moderate tender-mindedness (52nd pct) which shift against increased defense spending. The overall profile leans moderately opposed, around -412 on the anchored scale.
